title: Workplace Accommodations and Worker Functioning in Three Occupations (Social Security Bulletin v83n2)
description: Social Security Bulletin article (Vol. 83 No. 2; authors at the University of New Hampshire Institute on Disability) examining how workplace accommodations help workers with disabilities maintain or return to employment, based on a survey of 802 cashiers, receptionists, and nurses. Measures self-assessed physical and mental functioning (via WD-FAB domains) for workers who use accommodations versus those who do not need them, and discusses how an accommodation-availability measure could inform SSA disability evaluation. Relevant to vocational/functional assessment in DDS determinations.

source.publisher: U.S. Social Security Administration (SSA), Office of Retirement and Disability Policy
source.title: Social Security Bulletin, Vol. 83, No. 2 — Workplace Accommodations and Worker Functioning (University of New Hampshire Institute on Disability)

license: Not copyrighted (U.S. SSA publication; citation requested)
notes: Authoritative: a published research article in SSA's Social Security Bulletin (Vol. 83, No. 2), authored by University of New Hampshire Institute on Disability researchers under SSA grant RDR18000002, mirrored from ssa.gov/policy. The source URL is the canonical SSB v83n2 issue directory (article page is v83n2p17.html); deep article-page link not asserted. Survey reflects 2021 data with 2019 ACS comparison; issue date not stated cleanly in the scraped body so temporal_coverage is omitted. No tabular columns declared (the article includes survey tables, but the file is a narrative article).
